description: Mapping between NIST CSF 2.0 subcategories and SOC 2 TSC 2017 controls

mappings.source_control_name: Leadership is accountable for cybersecurity risk
mappings.notes: Both emphasize governance oversight and accountability for control performance.

mappings.source_control_name: Risk appetite and tolerance statements are established
mappings.notes: Both require leadership-defined risk tolerance and accountability expectations.

mappings.source_control_name: Specialized roles receive cybersecurity training
mappings.notes: CSF role-based training supports SOC2 competency objectives but does not fully cover performance management aspects.

mappings.source_control_name: Cybersecurity risk policy is established and enforced
mappings.notes: Policy communication and enforcement align with internal control communication needs.

mappings.source_control_name: Risk communication lines are established
mappings.notes: CSF communication includes external third-party risk channels, partially overlapping SOC2 external communication expectations.

mappings.source_control_name: Data inventories and metadata are maintained
mappings.notes: Data inventory supports information quality and documentation but does not fully represent SOC2 information flow governance.

mappings.source_control_name: Data-at-rest CIA protections
mappings.notes: Both require controls that protect sensitive data from unauthorized access or alteration.

mappings.source_control_name: Network integrity and segmentation protections
mappings.notes: Segmentation and boundary protection directly align with SOC2 system boundary controls.

mappings.source_control_name: Response plan execution
mappings.notes: CSF response-plan execution is one part of SOC2's broader detect-respond-recover incident lifecycle criterion.

mappings.source_control_name: Access permissions are policy-defined and enforced
mappings.notes: Least privilege and entitlement governance map directly to SOC2 access management requirements.

mappings.source_control_name: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated
mappings.notes: Both require robust user and system authentication mechanisms.

mappings.source_control_name: Physical access is managed and enforced
mappings.notes: Physical protection and monitoring expectations are directly aligned.

mappings.source_control_name: Monitoring for unauthorized users, devices, and software
mappings.notes: CSF continuous monitoring objective extends beyond baseline SOC2 monitoring and includes explicit unauthorized-activity detection scope.

mappings.source_control_name: Maintenance and repair are performed and logged
mappings.notes: Both require controlled, logged maintenance activities.

mappings.source_control_name: Backups are created, protected, maintained, and tested
mappings.notes: Backup lifecycle and testing requirements are closely aligned.

mappings.source_control_name: Credentials are issued, managed, revoked, and audited
mappings.notes: Identity lifecycle controls operationalize SOC2 logical access policy requirements.

mappings.source_control_name: Access authorizations follow least privilege and separation of duties
mappings.notes: Both require managed user access rights with review and constraints.

mappings.source_control_name: Entitlements and authorizations are managed and reviewed
mappings.notes: CSF entitlement governance partially covers privileged access controls but is not limited to privileged accounts.

mappings.source_control_name: Configuration management practices are established
mappings.notes: Configuration baseline and maintenance expectations are directly similar.

mappings.source_control_name: Secure software development practices are integrated
mappings.notes: CSF secure SDLC practices support software governance outcomes but SOC2 also emphasizes inventory and authorization.

mappings.source_control_name: Data-at-rest CIA protections
mappings.notes: Data-at-rest controls are a subset of broader SOC2 data lifecycle management obligations.

mappings.source_control_name: Configuration change control processes are in place
mappings.notes: Both require formal, authorized change management workflows.

mappings.source_control_name: Response and recovery plans are tested
mappings.notes: Plan testing is conceptually similar to pre-implementation testing rigor, but control intent differs.

mappings.source_control_name: New vulnerabilities are mitigated or risk-accepted
mappings.notes: Urgent mitigation workflows overlap with emergency change handling but are not strictly equivalent.

mappings.source_control_name: Threats, vulnerabilities, impacts, and likelihoods inform risk prioritization
mappings.notes: Both require systematic assessment and prioritization of risk.

mappings.source_control_name: Supplier and third-party risks are assessed and monitored
mappings.notes: Supplier risk governance aligns closely with SOC2 third-party risk control objectives.

mappings.source_control_name: Recovery plan is executed
mappings.notes: Execution of cyber recovery plans contributes to but does not fully represent enterprise continuity management.

mappings.source_control_name: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained
mappings.notes: Capacity and resilience safeguards support availability commitments but do not define service objectives alone.

mappings.source_control_name: Recovery plan is executed
mappings.notes: Both focus on maintaining and executing recovery capabilities for service restoration.

mappings.source_control_name: Integrity checking verifies software, firmware, and information integrity
mappings.notes: Integrity verification supports accurate processing outcomes but does not fully address end-to-end business processing logic.

mappings.source_control_name: Audit/log records are defined, implemented, and reviewed
mappings.notes: Logging and review practices map directly to SOC2 audit trail criteria.

mappings.source_control_name: Assets are prioritized using classification and criticality
mappings.notes: Asset classification and prioritization overlap with confidentiality classification but have broader asset scope.

mappings.source_control_name: Least-privilege access permissions are managed
mappings.notes: Least-privilege authorization directly supports restricting access to confidential information.

mappings.source_control_name: Legal and regulatory requirements (including privacy) are managed
mappings.notes: Compliance with privacy obligations supports notice requirements but does not itself define notice content.

mappings.source_control_name: Development and testing environments are separated from production
mappings.notes: Environment separation reduces misuse exposure for personal data processing but only partially addresses full use and retention obligations.
